issues.upper_principle_track: 信息地图与机器友好数据流要求 Canvas 是视觉影子，不能覆盖机器真相；系统升级必须协同要求调整 registry、view graph、canvas 文件和诊断记录；新视觉规则应优先表达正向层级模型。

issues.title: 项目根定位缺少机器契约，导致“业务/系统/知识工程系统”关系容易漂移
issues.failure_packet: 用户澄清：业务这个名词是临时说法，知识工程系统更符合项目定位；Codex 当前搭建的 agent 工作流一方面是作为工作流本身的系统搭建和维护知识工程系统功能，另一方面也是为了让知识工程系统真正运行闭环。此前系统没有独立机器契约承载这个根定位。

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: 讨论系统与业务关系时，现有文件只能从 README、架构文档、project-consumption 层和 Canvas 标题反推项目定位。
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: “业务”这个词不准只是表象；根因是项目根身份缺少机器真相，导致各模块只能用局部命名表达系统定位。

issues.lower_evidence_track: 从用户澄清回查：AGENTS/README 说知识网络底座，architecture 说知识网络架构，codex-integration 说认知底座，module-layer-model 有 project-consumption 层，但没有一个 system/project-ontology.json 定义知识工程系统、存在形式、生命周期和 Codex workflow 双重角色。
issues.upper_principle_track: 信息地图与机器友好数据流要求根定位不能只在聊天或 Markdown；系统升级必须协同要求同步 route、information map、topology、closure、visual coverage、Canvas 和人读说明；新系统模块必须对旧内容负责要求接管旧 README/docs/Canvas 定位表述。
issues.failed_mindset: module-from-surfaces thinking：从现有模块和图面反推项目本体，而不是先建立根定位契约再让模块分层从它派生。

issues.title: Canvas palette accepted named colors, causing loop graph style drift
issues.failure_packet: User reported that the loop module Canvas style looked wrong. Review found the loop view used plain named colors and the system overview retained a cyan shortcut alias while validation only checked palette membership.

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: The loop-orchestration Canvas had a different visual style from other current system views because it used purple/cyan/red/yellow/green/orange color tokens instead of stable Obsidian presets.
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: The incident is not just an unattractive loop graph. The deeper defect is that the governed color language accepted unstable token forms and did not scan same-class active views for token compatibility.

issues.lower_evidence_track: A current/active Canvas scan found named colors in loop-orchestration-architecture.canvas and cyan in kb-system-architecture.canvas. The loop_state palette itself listed named colors as allowed tokens.
issues.upper_principle_track: System upgrades must be coordinated across assets, registry, review, diagnostics and validation. Canvas is a visual shadow of machine truth, so visual semantics must be governed and machine-checkable rather than left as local styling.
issues.failed_mindset: Palette-membership-only thinking: treating any registered color string as safe without checking whether Obsidian Canvas renders that token consistently.
issues.immediate_root_cause: validate-canvas checked that node and edge colors belonged to the view palette, but did not validate the palette role colors or allowed_colors against stable Obsidian token formats.

root_problem.problem_statement: Canvas color semantics lacked a stable token compatibility contract.
root_problem.recurrence_mechanism: Any future palette could register plain named colors, pass validation, and produce inconsistent or unpolished Obsidian rendering across current/active views.
root_problem.owning_system_model: architecture-views visual semantic language, canvas-registry palettes and validate-canvas quality gate

issues.positive_model_repair: Define stable color tokens as part of the Canvas color semantic language: current/active palette roles, allowed_colors, node colors and edge colors must use Obsidian presets 1-6 or hex #RRGGBB. Migrate existing current/active views away from named colors and rerun visual audit.
issues.constraint_decision: Add a validation gate because the failure is machine-detectable and recurrence risk is moderate. No broad new negative-rule layer is added beyond stable token compatibility.

issues.title: Canvas visual acceptance overtrusted static validation after user-visible defect
issues.failure_packet: After the loop Canvas style repair, the user stated this was a serious system problem and asked for diagnosis. The earlier repair closed the named-color token defect, but the higher-layer concern is that user-visible Canvas defects repeatedly reach the user after prior validation or local closure claims.

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: A current loop Canvas view had a visible style problem, and an entry color legend had question-mark placeholder text that validation only exposed during the later repair run. The user had to classify the situation as serious for the diagnosis to move above local Canvas styling.
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: The incident is not just one bad loop graph or one corrupted legend. Those are evidence that the acceptance model was treating static validation as sufficient for user-visible visual quality.

issues.lower_evidence_track: Review found the local color-token defect and repaired it. During validation, validate-canvas also caught an existing entry color-legend question-run. Both show that user-visible Canvas quality can lag behind registry/palette logic and that validation results need to be tied to the original visual failure packet.
issues.upper_principle_track: Principle 1 requires diagnosing upward from local symptoms. Principle 2 requires coordinated upgrade across rules, assets, review, diagnostics and validation. The diagnostic self-audit policy already says validation passing while user-visible structure remains wrong is a diagnostic self-audit trigger.
issues.failed_mindset: validation-as-acceptance thinking: treating validate-canvas, audit-canvas-visual and text integrity as final visual acceptance instead of evidence that still needs comparison to the user-visible failure.
issues.immediate_root_cause: The Canvas and diagnostic contracts did not explicitly separate static validation evidence from user-visible visual acceptance at final response time, so a local validator fix could be reported as complete without naming rendered-view uncertainty.

root_problem.problem_statement: Canvas visual acceptance lacked an explicit boundary between static machine checks and user-visible rendered/readable acceptance.
root_problem.recurrence_mechanism: Future Canvas repairs can pass structural and geometry checks while still looking wrong in Obsidian or failing the user-visible intent, especially for style, hierarchy, spacing and render-specific behaviors.
root_problem.owning_system_model: architecture-views visual acceptance model, review-system visual criteria and diagnostic-system self-audit policy

diagnostic_self_audit.trigger: The user reclassified the loop Canvas style defect as a serious system problem after the local repair was summarized.
diagnostic_self_audit.missed_issue_pattern: The diagnosis initially focused on the immediate color-token gap and did not explicitly diagnose why post-validation user-visible defects keep requiring user detection.
diagnostic_self_audit.missed_layer: diagnosis acceptance boundary between static validation evidence and user-visible Canvas acceptance
diagnostic_self_audit.corrective_contract: For user-visible Canvas defects, final diagnosis must say whether acceptance checked the original visible concern and must record rendered-inspection limitations when no Obsidian screenshot/render pass was performed.
diagnostic_self_audit.recurrence_gate: Any future user correction saying a post-validation visual/structure repair is still wrong or serious triggers diagnostic_self_audit before closure.

issues.positive_model_repair: Upgrade Canvas acceptance from validator-only to a two-part model: static evidence from validators plus user-visible acceptance against the original failure packet. When rendered Obsidian inspection is unavailable, state the limitation and do not pretend the validator fully proves the visual experience.
issues.constraint_decision: Repair the positive acceptance model and add a diagnostic self-audit trigger. No broad visual-style prohibition is added; existing validators remain evidence gates, not the whole acceptance model.

issues.title: Canvas 缺少截图级视觉检查门禁
issues.failure_packet: 用户明确要求“需要有截图级别视觉检查”。现有 Canvas 检查虽然有 validate-canvas 和 audit-canvas-visual，但仍主要基于 JSON、登记和几何推断，没有为 current/active 图生成可复查的 PNG 截图证据。

issues.upper_principle_track: 符合“遇到问题先诊断”“系统升级必须协同”“信息地图与机器友好数据流”。事故表现是用户担心视觉审查不到截图层；根问题是视觉接受模型缺少 rendered evidence 层，不能只靠静态校验闭环。
issues.failed_mindset: static-evidence-only acceptance: 把 JSON/geometry 审查当成足够视觉证据，忽略用户实际看到的是渲染后的画面。
issues.immediate_root_cause: Canvas visual acceptance boundary 只登记了静态验证和用户可见接受原则，没有稳定工具产出截图证据，也未把截图工具接入 review、diagnostics、route 和 technical validation。

issues.constraint_decision: 采用正向模型升级，不增加针对某张图的负面规则。截图证据不是机器真相，只是视觉审查证据；Canvas registry 和 view graph 仍是结构真相。

issues.title: Canvas support files lacked machine-readable support semantics
issues.failure_packet: The user asked to learn from the OR project visual system. Comparison showed that OR uses registry-backed view graph, support relations and support bindings. This project already had readable file-label-* cards and screenshot audits, but canvas-registry support_relations/support_bindings were empty for all current and active structural views.

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: Current and active Canvas views had readable bottom support-file cards, but those cards did not declare which upper module, workflow step, status, boundary or gate they supported.
evidence_surface: system/canvas-registry.json before repair: support_relations/support_bindings all empty
scripts/validate-canvas.ps1 before repair did not enforce support semantics for existing views
scripts/audit-canvas-visual.ps1 before repair checked file-label cards but not support bindings
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: The surface symptom was not one missing line or one bad file card. The root issue was that the support layer had no registry-level upward ownership model, so agents could not interpret the bottom machine-truth band reliably.

issues.lower_evidence_track: Scanning current and active non-navigation views found 12 structural views with non-navigation support files. Those files had file-label-* human explanations, but registry support_relations/support_bindings were zero. The OR project provided a usable reference pattern for support relations and bindings.
issues.upper_principle_track: The information-map principle requires machine-friendly internal dataflow. Canvas is the visual shadow of the file system. New or upgraded modules must take responsibility for existing content, so the repair had to migrate existing current/active views instead of only adding a new rule.
issues.failed_mindset: visual-label-only thinking: treating a readable support-file card as enough structure while ignoring the agent need for layout-free, checkable and traceable support semantics.
issues.immediate_root_cause: The Canvas subsystem had upgraded support files into readable cards, but had not modeled card-to-upper-structure support as a registry contract with validation and visual-review gates.

root_problem.problem_statement: Canvas support-file bands lacked a registry-first support semantics model, so they could degrade into loose bibliography instead of checkable system structure.
root_problem.recurrence_mechanism: Future subsystem, workflow, schema, tool or knowledge maps would keep adding readable support cards without declaring their upper structural role, making omissions hard to review and hard for agents to use.
root_problem.owning_system_model: Canvas visual shadow system coordinated with canvas-registry, review-system and diagnostics

system_regulation_target.acceptance_gate: All current/active non-navigation Canvas support labels have support_bindings; validators and strict visual/screenshot audits pass.

issues.title: OR diagnostic module patterns need controlled adoption instead of surface copying
issues.failure_packet: User requested copying the powerful diagnostic-related modules from C:/Users/dzw/Desktop/or into this knowledge-engineering system. Direct copying would risk importing OR-specific research workflow, database assumptions and automatic self-evolution behavior without fitting this project's thin-entry, route-first and human-approved governance model.

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: The project already had a diagnostic module, but OR comparison showed missing layer-diagnosis matrix and controlled failure-learning loop objects: postmortem, proposal, regression guard and promotion decision.
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: The request to copy OR is an improvement signal. The deeper issue is missing method separation between diagnosis, controlled learning and actual rule promotion.

issues.lower_evidence_track: Compared OR diagnostic skill, layer matrix, system issue repair loop, P3 self-evolution design, failure loop service and migration tables against current diagnostic-system. Current system had failure packet, incident/root separation, generalization scan and self-audit, but lacked durable layer matrix and a controlled postmortem/proposal/regression/promotion loop.
issues.upper_principle_track: Principle 1 requires bottom-up diagnosis before repair. Principle 2 requires coordinated upgrades across routes, information-map, schema, validation, queue and Canvas. Principle 3 requires machine-friendly data rather than thick Markdown. Principle 4 requires upgraded modules to own existing diagnostic records and future issues.
issues.failed_mindset: surface-copy thinking: importing a strong external module by copying visible files or prose instead of extracting the reusable mechanism, adapting boundaries and registering it through machine truth.
issues.immediate_root_cause: The diagnostic subsystem did not yet separate diagnostic owner-layer selection from controlled failure learning and human-approved evolution promotion, so OR-style strength would have no stable contract or safe gate if copied directly.

root_problem.problem_statement: Diagnostics lacked a routed method surface for layer diagnosis and controlled failure learning, making repeated failures hard to convert into safe system evolution without bloating the skill or overfitting one incident.
root_problem.recurrence_mechanism: Future serious or repeated issues could be repaired and accepted locally without asking whether they should produce postmortem/proposal/regression/promotion objects, while OR-like rules might be added ad hoc to documents.
root_problem.owning_system_model: diagnostic-system coordinated with route-registry, information-map, diagnostic schema, validation, issue queue and Canvas visual projection

diagnostic_self_audit.trigger: External OR comparison revealed that current diagnostics could close repairs without a durable failure-learning decision.
diagnostic_self_audit.missed_issue_pattern: The diagnostic module had incident/root separation and same-class scan, but had not yet modeled repeated-failure learning as a separate controlled loop.
diagnostic_self_audit.missed_layer: controlled-failure-learning layer between diagnosis acceptance and durable rule or memory promotion
diagnostic_self_audit.corrective_contract: New non-trivial captured system_upgrade issues must decide whether to open, defer or skip the controlled failure-learning loop with reason.
diagnostic_self_audit.recurrence_gate: validate-diagnostics enforces the failure_learning_decision field from this issue onward.

issues.constraint_decision: Use a targeted validation gate because this is a durable diagnostic contract: from this issue onward captured system_upgrade records must include failure_learning_decision.status and reason. Do not force every issue to open postmortem/proposal objects.

issues.title: Knowledge entry front gate was too passive for authoritative knowledge discovery
issues.failure_packet: The user pointed at the first system-overview block, originally named capture and source registration, and clarified that this important module should become a knowledge-entry system that evaluates knowledge quality/value and proactively finds knowledge.

incident_manifestation.observed_incident: The visible first block only described incoming papers, GitHub, web pages and drafts entering inbox/source records.
incident_manifestation.why_it_is_not_root_problem: The wording of one Canvas card was only the visible symptom. The root problem was the absence of a registered front-gate decision layer for discovery and intake.

issues.lower_evidence_track: Route and information-map already had knowledge-ingestion, source types and quality precheck, but no child module, queue, route, closure proof or dedicated Canvas for proactive discovery and quality/value intake decisions.
issues.upper_principle_track: The system principles require diagnosis before repair, coordinated system upgrades, machine-friendly dataflow and new modules taking responsibility for existing content. The repair therefore had to update route, information map, topology, closure, visual coverage, Canvas and validators rather than only rename a block.
issues.failed_mindset: passive-capture thinking: assuming knowledge enters only after the user provides a source, instead of modeling the system responsibility to find authoritative candidates and decide if they deserve entry.
issues.immediate_root_cause: knowledge-ingestion lacked a child module for candidate discovery, quality/value scoring and intake decision before source registration.

root_problem.problem_statement: The knowledge-entry front gate did not have a durable positive model for proactive authoritative source discovery and intake decisions.
root_problem.recurrence_mechanism: Future important knowledge domains would keep relying on ad hoc user-provided sources or loose wording, making it hard to decide which first source is authoritative, worth storing or risky.
root_problem.owning_system_model: knowledge-ingestion coordinated with knowledge-acquisition, review-system, loop-orchestration, domain-maps and source-registry

issues.positive_model_repair: Create knowledge-acquisition as a child subsystem under knowledge-ingestion with machine truth, candidate queue, route, information-map registration, topology relation, activation proof, dedicated Canvas view, review handoff and validation gate.
issues.constraint_decision: Use a positive model upgrade rather than negative wording rules. The recurring issue is solved by a routed child system and candidate queue, not by forbidding old labels.

generalization_scan.observed_instance: The first visible entry block was too narrow for the actual knowledge-engineering need.
generalization_scan.failure_class: important system capability represented only as a passive stage card without its own machine contract, queue, route and visual child view
sibling_surfaces_checked: loop-orchestration remains durable repeatable loop owner
Canvas remains visual projection, not system truth
exemptions: No unattended crawler is enabled in this repair; external search remains triggered and subject to source quality/risk decisions.
No concrete source candidate is inserted as authoritative knowledge by this system upgrade.
generalization_scan.recurrence_gate: Future stable entry-stage capabilities must be checked for machine truth, route, queue/state if dynamic, visual coverage and closure proof before being treated as active.

system_regulation_target.update_mode: positive model upgrade: active knowledge acquisition and intake decision child subsystem
system_regulation_target.acceptance_gate: Validation passes after route, information map, topology, closure, visual coverage, Canvas registry/view graph and dedicated Canvas are synchronized.

failure_learning_decision.reason: This is a knowledge-ingestion capability upgrade, not a repeated diagnostic method failure. The recurrence gate is captured in diagnostics, and no separate failure-learning postmortem is needed unless the same front-gate activation failure recurs.

issues.diagnosis_acceptance: Accepted after the passive capture front gate was upgraded into an active knowledge-acquisition child subsystem with machine truth, candidate queue, route, information-map registration, topology, activation proof, visual coverage, dedicated Canvas and validation.
